title: Thresholded concurrency hedging to stabilize initial-token latency across multi-variant workloads
preview: Several enterprise customers across Hosted and Dedicated reported intermittent 200-600ms TTFT spikes and tokens/sec drop when model-variant fanout and tenant concurrency peak simultaneously. Investigation shows contention at the serving queue boundary combined with aggressive worker multiplexing: short-lived, high-priority requests preempt kernel scheduling and force warmstart/eviction churn for other inflight streams. Result: first-token latency spikes and sustained throughput collapse for some tenants and private deployments. This ticket implements a defensible, low-risk hedging layer that thresholds concurrency per tenant/model-variant and issues polite hedged requests (soft duplicate to warm pool) to avoid blocking kernel scheduling and reduce eviction storms. The goal is to regain median TTFT and stabilize tokens/sec within SLA while preserving throughput for bulk workloads.

title: Prompt Sentinel Matrix, Regression Severity Tiers, and Staged Rollback Choreography for Optimize v2
preview:
Purpose: Define a production-ready quality gating and rollback choreography for Optimize model rollouts. This ticket captures the PRD-level requirements for prompt sentinel composition, multi-tier regression severity rules, A/B criteria for canaries, and the automated/manual actions that should occur when signals cross thresholds. The goal is to make rollouts safe and actionable while minimizing unnecessary rollbacks and customer noise.

Background: Recent experiments with quantized model variants and batching changes produced subtle downstream quality regressions in targeted cohorts. Current guardrails are ad-hoc (manual dashboards and pager alerts) and do not codify graduated responses. This PRD standardizes signal definitions and explicit remediation steps so Product, SRE, and Runtime can automate first-line mitigations and surface only actionable incidents to on-call.

Scope:
- Design prompt sentinel sampling matrix (core sentinels, exploratory, adversarial probes) and provenance labels.
- Define composite quality score and per-cohort metrics (accuracy proxy, hallucination rate, instruction-following, toxicity score).
- Specify multi-tier regression thresholds (warning/critical/blocking) with statistical tests and minimum sample sizes.
- Define A/B canary rules: when to stop, pause, or rollback.
- Orchestrate staged rollback choreography (automated rollback, traffic-shaping, model variant fallback).

Out of scope:
- New instrumentation implementations (this ticket defines requirements; separate eng tickets will implement metrics export).
- UI/console design for dashboards (tracked in a separate design ticket).

title: Conversation framing sanitizer and token-stream alignment for pinned model rollouts
preview: Summary: New model bundles arriving from third-party contributors include inconsistent conversation framing (role delimiters, invisible separators, and control tokens) that leads to subtle tokenization mismatches when we pin model versions and publish performance profiles. This ticket implements a conservative 'conversation framing sanitizer' in the onboarding pipeline that normalizes role markers, strips invisible separators, and produces a canonical token-stream preview used to validate performance profile publishing and runtime pinning. Motivation: Without normalization, customers see token-level regressions after pinning (token counts differ from onboarding benchmark), and the profile publish job can publish misleading per-token latency/cost metrics. Goal: ensure the onboarding preview and published profile match the runtime tokenization the serving stack will produce for pinned models, and add safety checks to prevent profile publish if divergence > 3% or if unknown control tokens are present.

title: Staged Pin Eviction & Regional Variant Escalation with Observability Hooks
preview: Goal: design and implement a staged eviction and fallback mechanism for model version pins that minimizes customer disruption during deprecations or regional capacity disruptions. This ticket covers: 1) pin metadata TTL and staged eviction windows, 2) a priority escalation path for regional/model-variant fallback, 3) telemetry hooks and SLO signals that drive automated grace windows, and 4) compatibility checks for variant fallbacks (quantization/hardware compatibility enforcement). Success criteria: (a) zero user-visible 5xxs during a controlled pin eviction across 3 customers in staging, (b) bounded tail latency increase <= 15% for affected requests during an eviction grace window, and (c) clear rollback path and audit trail for every automated fallback. Non-goals: making permanent changes to pricing, or modifying model catalog discovery flows beyond adding compatibility metadata fields.

title: Per-language repair playbook for SDK errors and streaming recovery
preview: Problem: SDK consumers struggle to map low-level runtime failures to concrete remediation steps. Streaming operations compound the issue because partial progress is often lost and client authors are uncertain when to resume, retry, or fallback. This ticket delivers a per-language (Python/TypeScript/Go) "repair playbook": standardized error envelope, stable error codes, short human-readable recovery blurb, and language-specific quick-fix snippets for common failure classes (transient network, upstream model OOM, auth/KMS failures, and stream checkpoint/resume hints). The goal is consistent developer UX across SDKs and measurable reduction in support tickets and mean time to recovery for production incidents.

title: Fallback priority graph and staggered threshold calibrator
preview:
Problem: We need a deterministic, testable mechanism to decide when to trip per-dependency / per-model / per-tenant circuits and how to pick safe fallbacks. The existing heuristics are ad-hoc (static error thresholds + simple decay) and have produced either noisy trips for low-traffic tenants or slow reactions to cascading latency spikes in multi-region failover scenarios.

Goal: Build a calibration and orchestration artifact that: 1) generates a prioritized graph of fallback destinations for each (tenant, model, region) tuple (eg: same-model-quantized, smaller-model, cached-prefix, no-op/400), 2) computes staggered trip thresholds that account for traffic volume and downstream dependency criticality, and 3) provides a simulation harness to validate threshold choices against historical traffic and injected failures so we can pick thresholds that minimize SLO breaches while bounding cost.

Scope: Implement the priority graph DSL, the threshold-calibrator service (offline + realtime scoring), a simulator to run candidate configs against historical traces, and runtime integration hooks that expose the chosen thresholds to the gateway and to the Circuit Control Plane. This ticket covers design, simulator, and a canary rollout plan. Implementation of runtime enforcement is tracked in ENG-70122 (runtime wiring).

title: Dedicated cluster post-incident: allocator tuning, noisy-neighbor attribution, and burst smoothing
preview: Summary: A dedicated-cluster capacity event led to elevated evictions and degraded tail latency for multiple tenants during a traffic surge on 2026-02-09. Root cause analysis shows a confluence of (1) an unexpectedly noisy tenant with sustained KV/prefix cache pressure, (2) reservation mis-sizing for a subset of pools (under-provisioned headroom), and (3) burst token logic that allowed short-term oversubscription without adequate backoff. The incident surfaced limits in our allocator heuristics and insufficient attribution telemetry to quickly identify the noisy workload. This ticket tracks the post-incident action items: allocator tuning, noisy-neighbor attribution, reservation reconciliation, burst smoothing, and improved alerting/playbooks.

title: Add telemetry for marketplace onboarding funnel (entitlement → org link → API key → first request)
preview:
Goal: reduce time-to-first-success for AWS/GCP Marketplace buyers by instrumenting the end-to-end onboarding funnel and identifying drop-offs.

We need consistent, debuggable telemetry across:
1) entitlement lookup latency + outcomes
2) org linking success/failure (including existing org edge cases)
3) API key creation completion
4) first successful API request after provisioning

This ticket adds event definitions, implementation hooks (console + backend), and the first dashboards/alerts so GTM/Eng can monitor launch readiness and regressions.

Primary questions we want to answer:
- Where are users dropping? (entitlement check vs org link vs API key vs first request)
- How long does entitlement verification take by provider (AWS vs GCP) and by error type?
- How often do entitlement propagation delays happen vs true configuration errors?
- What’s median and p95 time from subscription → org provisioned → first token?

Non-goals:
- Building a full growth analytics framework for all onboarding paths (this is marketplace-specific).
- Logging any sensitive payloads (no API keys, tokens, prompts, or PII beyond required identifiers).

title: API Stability Contract & Client Migration Timeline for Legacy Generate/Stream Endpoints
preview: Executive summary: This PRD defines the contract and timeline for phasing out the legacy /v0/generate and multiplexed streaming endpoints and guiding clients to the unified inference API. The deliverable is a prescriptive migration timeline, stability guarantees for each phase (SLO windows and compatibility guarantees), clear acceptance criteria for SDKs and server behavior, and a customer-facing migration playbook. The output will enable predictable client upgrades while protecting production traffic via circuit breakers, telemetry gates, and rollback paths.

title: Lifecycle growth experiments sprint plan (Hosted API self-serve activation + early retention)
preview:
## Summary
4-week cross-functional sprint to improve Hosted API self-serve activation + early retention via lifecycle email, in-console onboarding nudges, and paid retargeting. This ticket is the source of truth for milestones, owners, guardrails, and weekly status.

Primary outcomes:
- Activation lift: % of new signups completing (a) first successful request and (b) first 100k tokens within 7 days
- Reduce time-to-first-token (TTFT) / time-to-value
- Improve D7/D30 active usage; improve downstream conversion signals (upgrade intent + sales-assisted handoff signals where appropriate)

## Owners / RACI
- Experiment owner (overall): Ben Carter (Growth/Marketing)
- Exec sponsor: Camila Reyes
- Product surface (Console nudges): Marissa Cole
- Telemetry/rollouts input: Paula Mendes
- Eng lead (Console & tooling): Dylan Brooks
- Messaging review: Soojin Lee
- Marketing ops + attribution: Tyler Benson
- Sales audience exclusions/handoff rules: Jordan Blake / Stephanie Nguyen
- Privacy/compliance: Michael Grant (CAN-SPAM) + Olga Petrov (GDPR)

## Links (artifacts)
- Sprint brief (Confluence): sources/confluence/product-docs/requirements/lifecycle-growth-experiments-sprint-brief.json
- Email onboarding drip v2 spec: sources/confluence/product-docs/design-specs/email-onboarding-drip-v2.json

- Messaging guidelines (Hosted API): sources/confluence/sales-enablement/positioning-and-messaging/onboarding-messaging-guidelines-hosted-api.json
- Data handling policy note: sources/confluence/policies-and-process/data-management/lifecycle-email-retargeting-data-handling.json
- Experiment backlog (ICE scoring): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/lifecycle-growth-sprint-experiment-backlog.json
- Activation metrics definitions + SQL notes: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/activation-metrics-definition-and-sql-notes.json
- Event tracking spec (Eng draft): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/engineering/console-and-tooling/event-tracking-spec-lifecycle-growth.json
- Activation funnel dashboard spec: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/engineering/observability/activation-funnel-dashboard-spec.json
- Readout template: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/experiment-readout-template.json

Engineering implementation references:
- PR: add activation events: sources/github/redwood/pr-28431-add-activation-events-for-onboarding-funnel.json
- PR: console nudge component + targeting: sources/github/redwood/pr-28458-console-nudge-component-and-targeting-rules.json
- PR: lifecycle growth feature flags + holdout: sources/github/redwood/pr-28477-add-feature-flag-lifecycle-growth-experiments.json
- PR: email preferences/unsubscribe in console: sources/github/redwood/pr-28505-email-preferences-unsubscribe-link-in-console.json
- PR: fix duplicate signup event emission: sources/github/redwood/pr-28521-fix-duplicate-signup-event-emission.json

## Hypotheses
1) Email onboarding sequencing with clearer “next step” CTAs + code snippets will reduce drop-off between signup → API key → first request.
2) In-console “next best action” nudges will decrease time-to-first-token and increase completion of key setup steps.
3) Conservative retargeting (signup-no-activation, activated-but-no-return) will be cost-efficient and additive to activation/retention without polluting sales-assisted attribution.

## Scope (in)
Email
- Drip v2 triggers + segmentation (new users, stalled users, first-success users)
- Copy variants + send schedule + ramp plan
- Deliverability monitoring and escalation
- Compliance: unsubscribe + suppression rules

Console
- Feature-flagged nudges (API key creation, first request snippet, enable streaming, view usage dashboard)
- Targeting rules + holdout approach
- Dismissal persistence + UX QA

Retargeting
- Audience definitions + exclusions for active opps / enterprise domains
- UTM conventions + landing page mapping
- Spend caps + CPA monitoring + creative iterations

Measurement
- Event taxonomy + dedupe rules + consistent funnel definitions
- Activation funnel dashboard + alerting for regressions
- Weekly readouts + final consolidated recommendation (keep/iterate/kill)

## Out of scope (explicit)
- Pricing changes
- Large docs replatforming
- Major product re-architecture
- Deep SDK rewrites beyond small onboarding-error improvements

## Success metrics + decision criteria
Primary:
- Activation rate: first successful request within 7 days (signup cohort)
- Activation depth: first 100k tokens within 7 days

Secondary:
- TTFT distribution (median + P90), time-to-first-request
- D7/D30 active usage (cohort-based)
- Support load related to onboarding (tickets tagged “onboarding” / “email deliverability”)

Decision guidelines (per experiment):
- Ship/expand if we see directional lift with acceptable guardrails (deliverability, complaint rate, support load) and no instrumentation concerns.
- Iterate if signal is promising but bottleneck is copy/targeting/landing page.
- Kill if negative lift or operational risk outweighs upside.

## Guardrails (must-haves)
- CAN-SPAM/GDPR: clear unsubscribe, suppression honored across sends and surfaces
- Conservative retargeting: exclude active sales opps + sensitive accounts; avoid targeting currently active users
- Deliverability: throttle ramps; monitor bounce/complaint rates; pause thresholds owned by Ben Carter + Tyler Benson
- Experiment integrity: consistent event definitions; enforce holdouts where possible; fix dedupe issues before interpreting results

## Milestones + weekly plan
Week 1 (Jan 13–Jan 19): Metrics + email v2 launch
- Finalize KPI definitions + event taxonomy (PM-651, ENG-10492)
- Finalize Email v2 copy + templates (PM-659, DES-311)
- Launch first email A/B with ramp plan (Ben + Tyler)
Deliverables:
- KPI spec + query notes updated
- Email v2 live for initial cohort(s)

Week 2 (Jan 20–Jan 26): Console nudge framework + first nudge experiment
- Ship nudge component + targeting/holdout (ENG-10501, PM-663, DES-318)
- QA: dismissal persistence + page eligibility
- Start nudge experiment 1: “Create API key” + “Run first request”
Deliverables:
- Nudge framework in production behind flags
- Nudge experiment 1 running with dashboard tracking

Week 3 (Jan 27–Feb 2): Retargeting launch
- Audience definitions + exclusions (PM-670 + Sales alignment)
- Creative kit shipped (DES-327)
- Launch with spend cap + UTMs
Deliverables:
- Retargeting live; daily spend/CPA monitoring

Week 4 (Feb 3–Feb 7): Consolidated analysis + recommendations
- Consolidated analysis across email + nudges + retargeting
- Recommendations: always-on vs iterate vs stop
- Follow-on backlog with owners
Deliverables:
- Final sprint readout + next sprint backlog

## Risks / mitigations
- Instrumentation skew (duplicate events, regional gaps)
  - Mitigation: validate dashboard vs raw event tables; block readout conclusions until dedupe fix confirmed (ENG-10492 + ENG-10533)
- Deliverability degradation from volume increase
  - Mitigation: ramp + monitoring; shared risk log; pause thresholds; DKIM/SPF/DMARC check (INT-3301)
- Retargeting attribution contamination / sales conflict
  - Mitigation: explicit suppression list + weekly sync with Sales ops; documented rules in audience defs
- Console UX regressions (nudges blocking flows)
  - Mitigation: QA checklist + rapid rollback via feature flags; support escalation path

## Comms cadence
- Weekly 30m office hours (Growth + Product + Eng + Sales) for blockers/decisions
- Weekly readout using template: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/experiment-readout-template.json
- Daily asynchronous updates in #marketing-growth + #product-console during launches

- Email preferences/unsubscribe link shipped in console (PR-28505), reducing compliance risk and support burden.

text:
## Summary
4-week cross-functional sprint to improve Hosted API self-serve activation + early retention via lifecycle email, in-console onboarding nudges, and paid retargeting. This ticket is the source of truth for milestones, owners, guardrails, and weekly status.

Primary outcomes:
- Activation lift: % of new signups completing (a) first successful request and (b) first 100k tokens within 7 days
- Reduce time-to-first-token (TTFT) / time-to-value
- Improve D7/D30 active usage; improve downstream conversion signals (upgrade intent + sales-assisted handoff signals where appropriate)

## Owners / RACI
- Experiment owner (overall): Ben Carter (Growth/Marketing)
- Exec sponsor: Camila Reyes
- Product surface (Console nudges): Marissa Cole
- Telemetry/rollouts input: Paula Mendes
- Eng lead (Console & tooling): Dylan Brooks
- Messaging review: Soojin Lee
- Marketing ops + attribution: Tyler Benson
- Sales audience exclusions/handoff rules: Jordan Blake / Stephanie Nguyen
- Privacy/compliance: Michael Grant (CAN-SPAM) + Olga Petrov (GDPR)

## Links (artifacts)
- Sprint brief (Confluence): sources/confluence/product-docs/requirements/lifecycle-growth-experiments-sprint-brief.json
- Email onboarding drip v2 spec: sources/confluence/product-docs/design-specs/email-onboarding-drip-v2.json
- Console nudges framework spec: sources/confluence/product-docs/design-specs/console-onboarding-nudges-experiment-framework.json
- Messaging guidelines (Hosted API): sources/confluence/sales-enablement/positioning-and-messaging/onboarding-messaging-guidelines-hosted-api.json
- Data handling policy note: sources/confluence/policies-and-process/data-management/lifecycle-email-retargeting-data-handling.json
- Experiment backlog (ICE scoring): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/lifecycle-growth-sprint-experiment-backlog.json
- Activation metrics definitions + SQL notes: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/activation-metrics-definition-and-sql-notes.json
- Event tracking spec (Eng draft): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/engineering/console-and-tooling/event-tracking-spec-lifecycle-growth.json
- Activation funnel dashboard spec: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/engineering/observability/activation-funnel-dashboard-spec.json
- Readout template: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/experiment-readout-template.json

Engineering implementation references:
- PR: add activation events: sources/github/redwood/pr-28431-add-activation-events-for-onboarding-funnel.json
- PR: console nudge component + targeting: sources/github/redwood/pr-28458-console-nudge-component-and-targeting-rules.json
- PR: lifecycle growth feature flags + holdout: sources/github/redwood/pr-28477-add-feature-flag-lifecycle-growth-experiments.json
- PR: email preferences/unsubscribe in console: sources/github/redwood/pr-28505-email-preferences-unsubscribe-link-in-console.json
- PR: fix duplicate signup event emission: sources/github/redwood/pr-28521-fix-duplicate-signup-event-emission.json

## Hypotheses
1) Email onboarding sequencing with clearer “next step” CTAs + code snippets will reduce drop-off between signup → API key → first request.
2) In-console “next best action” nudges will decrease time-to-first-token and increase completion of key setup steps.
3) Conservative retargeting (signup-no-activation, activated-but-no-return) will be cost-efficient and additive to activation/retention without polluting sales-assisted attribution.

## Scope (in)
Email
- Drip v2 triggers + segmentation (new users, stalled users, first-success users)
- Copy variants + send schedule + ramp plan
- Deliverability monitoring and escalation
- Compliance: unsubscribe + suppression rules

Console
- Feature-flagged nudges (API key creation, first request snippet, enable streaming, view usage dashboard)
- Targeting rules + holdout approach
- Dismissal persistence + UX QA

Retargeting
- Audience definitions + exclusions for active opps / enterprise domains
- UTM conventions + landing page mapping
- Spend caps + CPA monitoring + creative iterations

Measurement
- Event taxonomy + dedupe rules + consistent funnel definitions
- Activation funnel dashboard + alerting for regressions
- Weekly readouts + final consolidated recommendation (keep/iterate/kill)

## Out of scope (explicit)
- Pricing changes
- Large docs replatforming
- Major product re-architecture
- Deep SDK rewrites beyond small onboarding-error improvements

## Success metrics + decision criteria
Primary:
- Activation rate: first successful request within 7 days (signup cohort)
- Activation depth: first 100k tokens within 7 days

Secondary:
- TTFT distribution (median + P90), time-to-first-request
- D7/D30 active usage (cohort-based)
- Support load related to onboarding (tickets tagged “onboarding” / “email deliverability”)

Decision guidelines (per experiment):
- Ship/expand if we see directional lift with acceptable guardrails (deliverability, complaint rate, support load) and no instrumentation concerns.
- Iterate if signal is promising but bottleneck is copy/targeting/landing page.
- Kill if negative lift or operational risk outweighs upside.

## Guardrails (must-haves)
- CAN-SPAM/GDPR: clear unsubscribe, suppression honored across sends and surfaces
- Conservative retargeting: exclude active sales opps + sensitive accounts; avoid targeting currently active users
- Deliverability: throttle ramps; monitor bounce/complaint rates; pause thresholds owned by Ben Carter + Tyler Benson
- Experiment integrity: consistent event definitions; enforce holdouts where possible; fix dedupe issues before interpreting results

## Milestones + weekly plan
Week 1 (Jan 13–Jan 19): Metrics + email v2 launch
- Finalize KPI definitions + event taxonomy (PM-651, ENG-10492)
- Finalize Email v2 copy + templates (PM-659, DES-311)
- Launch first email A/B with ramp plan (Ben + Tyler)
Deliverables:
- KPI spec + query notes updated
- Email v2 live for initial cohort(s)

Week 2 (Jan 20–Jan 26): Console nudge framework + first nudge experiment
- Ship nudge component + targeting/holdout (ENG-10501, PM-663, DES-318)
- QA: dismissal persistence + page eligibility
- Start nudge experiment 1: “Create API key” + “Run first request”
Deliverables:
- Nudge framework in production behind flags
- Nudge experiment 1 running with dashboard tracking

Week 3 (Jan 27–Feb 2): Retargeting launch
- Audience definitions + exclusions (PM-670 + Sales alignment)
- Creative kit shipped (DES-327)
- Launch with spend cap + UTMs
Deliverables:
- Retargeting live; daily spend/CPA monitoring

Week 4 (Feb 3–Feb 7): Consolidated analysis + recommendations
- Consolidated analysis across email + nudges + retargeting
- Recommendations: always-on vs iterate vs stop
- Follow-on backlog with owners
Deliverables:
- Final sprint readout + next sprint backlog

## Risks / mitigations
- Instrumentation skew (duplicate events, regional gaps)
  - Mitigation: validate dashboard vs raw event tables; block readout conclusions until dedupe fix confirmed (ENG-10492 + ENG-10533)
- Deliverability degradation from volume increase
  - Mitigation: ramp + monitoring; shared risk log; pause thresholds; DKIM/SPF/DMARC check (INT-3301)
- Retargeting attribution contamination / sales conflict
  - Mitigation: explicit suppression list + weekly sync with Sales ops; documented rules in audience defs
- Console UX regressions (nudges blocking flows)
  - Mitigation: QA checklist + rapid rollback via feature flags; support escalation path

## Comms cadence
- Weekly 30m office hours (Growth + Product + Eng + Sales) for blockers/decisions
- Weekly readout using template: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/experiment-readout-template.json
- Daily asynchronous updates in #marketing-growth + #product-console during launches

### 2026-02-02 (Week 3 readout)

### 2026-02-07 (Final)

  - Email v2: keep winner subject + CTA pattern; iterate stalled-user branch.
  - Nudges: graduate “Run first request” nudge to always-on for new signups; keep “Enable streaming” behind flag for iteration.
  - Retargeting: keep activated-but-no-return audience; reduce spend on signup-no-activation until landing page is simplified.

title: Retargeting experiment plan + budget (Lifecycle growth sprint)
preview:
## Summary
Plan + launch checklist for Week 3 retargeting as part of the Lifecycle growth experiments sprint. This ticket consolidates:
- Audience definitions + lookback windows
- Budget caps + pacing rules
- UTM conventions + landing page mapping
- Exclusion rules (active Sales opps + sensitive accounts) and operational process

## Goals / success criteria
Primary: incremental lift in activation for the Hosted API without polluting Sales/enterprise pipeline.
- Conversion event: new signup that reaches **first successful request** within 7 days of signup.
- Secondary: first 100k tokens within 7 days, D7 return rate.
- Paid metrics (sanity): CTR, CPC, CPA-to-signup, CPA-to-activated.

Decision threshold (Week 4 readout):
- Keep/scale if we see a consistent activation lift vs holdout/baseline AND CPA-to-activated is within target band.
- Kill/pare back if CPA-to-activated is out of band or if we can’t enforce exclusions cleanly.

## Channels (initial)
1) Google Display (incl. YouTube remarketing if creative allows)
2) LinkedIn retargeting (focus: engineering job functions)
3) Optional: Reddit retargeting (only if exclusions + tracking hold up; lower priority)

## Audience definitions (v1)
All audiences must exclude:
- Existing paying customers / accounts with active contract (Hosted/Dedicated/Private)
- Current active users: anyone with API usage in last 7 days (avoid paying to reach already-active devs)
- Suppressed Sales opps + sensitive accounts (see Exclusions)

### A1: Site visitors (non-signup)
Intent: capture high-intent docs/quickstart visitors.
- Include: visitors to /docs, /pricing, /models, /benchmarks in last 14 days
- Exclude: visited /contact-sales or /enterprise pages (we don’t want to step on enterprise motion)
- Notes: require cookie consent; if consent missing, do not include.

### A2: Signup, no activation (highest priority)
Intent: bring back developers who created an account but didn’t reach first request.
- Include: signed up in last 21 days
- AND no event for first_successful_request within 7 days of signup
- Suggested sub-segments:
  - A2a: no API key created
  - A2b: API key created but no first request

### A3: Activated once, no return
Intent: early retention nudge (get them to 100k tokens / second session).
- Include: first_successful_request occurred, but no activity in last 7 days
- Lookback: 30 days
- Exclude: accounts tagged as “Sales assisted” or with active opp

### A4: Install intent (SDK / quickstart interaction)
Intent: target devs that engaged with quickstarts but didn’t convert.
- Include: visited quickstart pages or GitHub quickstart outbound click tracked in last 14 days
- Exclude: already signed up (to avoid competing with A2 messaging)

## Landing pages + message mapping
Landing pages should match cohort state and reduce time-to-first-token.

LP1 (default): Docs Quickstart
- URL: /docs/quickstart
- Primary CTA: “Make your first request”
- Use for A1 + A4.

LP2: Console onboarding checklist (signed-in)
- URL: /console/onboarding (or equivalent in-app deep link)
- Primary CTA: “Create API key” / “Run first request” depending on state
- Use for A2 + A3.

LP3: Minimal ‘Hello world’ snippet (fast activation)
- URL: /quickstarts/hello-world (or canonical docs page)
- Use for A2a / A2b when the ad platform supports deep link + we can pass through state.

Guardrail: do NOT use /contact-sales as a default landing page for these cohorts.

## Budget + pacing (Week 3)
Approved spend cap proposal for Week 3 launch window:
- Total cap: **$12,000** for initial 7-day run
- Daily cap: **$1,500/day** combined across platforms
- Pacing:
  - Day 1–2: 30% of daily cap (learning + verification)
  - Day 3–7: scale up to full daily cap if exclusions + tracking are clean

Allocation (initial):
- Google Display: 50%
- LinkedIn: 40%
- Reddit (optional): 10% (only after we validate suppression + attribution)

Frequency caps:
- 2 impressions/day/user (LinkedIn)
- 3 impressions/day/user (Google) with 7-day max frequency cap where possible

## UTM conventions
Standard format:
- utm_source: google | linkedin | reddit
- utm_medium: paid_social | display
- utm_campaign: lgx_sprint_w3_retargeting
- utm_content: {audience}-{creative}-{variant}
- utm_term: optional for platform keyword/placement buckets

Examples:
- ?utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=paid_social&utm_campaign=lgx_sprint_w3_retargeting&utm_content=a2b-consolechecklist-v1
- ?utm_source=google&utm_medium=display&utm_campaign=lgx_sprint_w3_retargeting&utm_content=a1-docsquickstart-v2

Naming rules:
- Audience code must be present (a1/a2a/a2b/a3/a4)
- Creative version increments whenever headline/body changes materially

## Exclusions / suppression rules (Sales + privacy guardrails)
Hard exclusions (must be enforced before launch):
1) Active Sales opportunities (HubSpot)
- Exclude companies/domains with an open opp in stages: Discovery, Evaluation, Security Review, Procurement, Contracting
- Exclude any account flagged as “Strategic / Enterprise motion”
- Refresh cadence: daily (automated) + weekly manual spot-check with Sales Ops

2) Sensitive / regulated accounts
- Exclude: known regulated prospects (e.g., fintech/healthcare) where retargeting is disallowed by customer request or policy
- Exclude list owner: Jordan Blake / Isabelle Laurent (Sales Ops)

3) Internal + competitors
- Exclude internal domains + known competitor domains from audience building

Operational process:
- Sales provides a weekly exported suppression list (domains + company IDs), stored in the shared drive and ingested by Marketing Ops.
- Tyler Benson owns the ingestion + validation in ad platforms.

## Measurement + attribution notes
- Primary reporting: activation funnel dashboard (signup → api_key_created → first_successful_request → first_100k_tokens)
- Breakdown dimensions:
  - utm_source / utm_campaign / utm_content
  - audience code
  - region

Attribution window (initial):
- 7-day click / 1-day view (platform), but we will rely on first-party eventing for core activation counts.

Data quality checks before scaling spend:
- Confirm UTM parameters persist through redirect + console sign-in
- Confirm events are not missing for EU region (known discrepancy risk)
- Confirm holdout / baseline comparison is readable (at minimum: pre/post + matched cohorts)

## Launch checklist
Pre-launch (T-2 days):
- [ ] Confirm creative exports from DES-327
- [ ] Confirm audiences configured with exclusions applied (Jordan/Isabelle sign-off)
- [ ] Confirm landing pages match ad claims + have correct CTAs
- [ ] Confirm UTM QA in each platform
- [ ] Confirm dashboards updated and alert thresholds set (ENG-10533)

Launch day:
- [ ] Start at 30% daily cap
- [ ] Verify spend + traffic + conversion events by EOD
- [ ] Validate no traffic to suppressed domains via spot check

Post-launch:
- [ ] Daily: CPA-to-signup + volume + obvious targeting errors
- [ ] Twice weekly: activation lift + cohort quality

## Owners
- Experiment owner: Ben Carter
- Marketing Ops + attribution: Tyler Benson
- Sales exclusions + handoff rules: Jordan Blake / Stephanie Nguyen / Isabelle Laurent
- Exec sponsor: Camila Reyes

## Open questions
1) Do we add a separate audience for “Visited /models or /benchmarks” vs general docs visitors?
2) Do we allow retargeting in EEA by default or require a stricter consent gate? (Need Olga/Michael guidance if ambiguous.)
3) Do we want separate messaging variants emphasizing latency vs cost vs reliability for A1 vs A2?

## Final outcome
Week 3 launch completed with conservative pacing; exclusions and UTMs validated; results summarized in the Week 3 readout doc and used in the consolidated sprint readout.

title: Retargeting creative kit (Lifecycle Growth Sprint wk3): dev-first variants + exports
preview:
## Context
We’re launching Week 3 retargeting as part of the **Lifecycle Growth Experiments Sprint** (Hosted API). This ticket produces a small, reusable **retargeting creative kit** for warm audiences (site visitors / signup-no-activation / activated-no-return).

Primary goal: downstream activation lift (First successful request within 7 days), not CTR-only.

## Audience stages (creative should map to state)
- **A: Site visitor (no signup):** “Come back and try it” → quickstart/product page.
- **B: Signup, no activation:** “Finish setup / run minimal request” → console login / quickstart.
- **C: Activated, no return:** “Next value step” → usage dashboard / streaming docs.

## Messaging guardrails (must follow)
- Dev-first, concrete, minimal hype.
- Avoid unverified quantitative claims (latency/cost/uplift). Use directional language ("built for", "designed to").
- Avoid personalization language like “We noticed you…”.
- No enterprise-heavy positioning as the primary headline for self-serve cohorts.
- Ensure CTAs match landing pages exactly ("Get started", "Finish setup", "Open Console", etc.).

Reference: onboarding messaging guidelines + retargeting creatives brief.

## Deliverables
### Concepts (minimum)
Deliver **3 concepts**, each with **2 copy variants** (v1/v2), sized for core placements.

Concept 1 — **Activation flow (Audience B)**
- Visual: simple 3-step flow diagram: Signup → API key → First request
- Copy direction: “Make your first successful request in minutes” / “Finish setup: create an API key”
- CTA: Finish setup / Make first request

Concept 2 — **Cost + latency visibility (Audience C)**
- Visual: product/console dashboard-style frame (illustrative; avoid unreadable micro-text)
- Copy direction: “See token usage, latency, and errors per route”
- CTA: Open Console / View dashboard

- Visual: text-forward creative with a readable curl or TypeScript snippet
- Copy direction: “Hosted LLM inference you can ship” / “Copy/paste a minimal request”
- CTA: Get started / View quickstart

- Visual: terminal-style “streaming output” lines, high contrast
- Copy direction: “See tokens sooner with streaming”
- CTA: Try streaming

### Sizes
- 1200×628 (landscape)
- 1080×1080 (square)
- 1080×1920 (story) — only for concepts that remain readable in vertical crops

### Export + handoff requirements
- Final exports: PNG (preferred) or JPG, < 1.5MB when possible.
- Provide a consistent naming scheme that matches UTM `utm_content` keys.
- Provide source file link (Figma) with components organized by concept/variant/size.

## File naming (must match attribution/UTM)
Format: `{concept}_{variant}_{size}`
- concept: activationflow | costlatency | hostedapi | streaming
- variant: v1 | v2
- size: 1200x628 | 1080x1080 | 1080x1920

Examples:
- activationflow_v1_1200x628.png
- hostedapi_v2_1080x1080.png

## Acceptance criteria
- At least **6 static creatives** total (3 concepts × 2 variants) delivered in **1200×628 + 1080×1080**.
- At least **one “pure dev” text-forward** variant (snippet-heavy, minimal decoration).
- Text legible on mobile (no tiny subcopy/disclaimers).
- Redwood logo present; product labeled “Redwood API” or “Hosted API”.
- No stock robot/brain visuals.
- Messaging review sign-off (Soojin) + Ops QA readiness (Tyler) before upload.

## Review plan
1) Internal design review (Tessa) — layout + readability.
2) Messaging review (Soojin) — claims + tone + CTA alignment.
3) Growth/Ops QA (Ben/Tyler) — naming/UTMs + platform constraints.

- Whether to include any “Hosted → Dedicated/Private later” line: default is **NO** unless there’s whitespace and it doesn’t steal focus from activation.

title: Refresh onboarding email template (v2): layout, typography, and mobile responsiveness
preview:
Update the HTML email template used for the Hosted API onboarding drip v2 to improve readability, copy fit, and mobile responsiveness across common clients (Gmail, iOS Mail, Outlook). Ensure the template supports A/B subject + headline tests, dynamic sections (by segment), and compliance/footer requirements.

Context
- This template will be used across Week 1 email A/B and subsequent iterations.
- Must align with messaging guidelines: performance + reliability, avoid over-claiming, keep copy developer-first.
- Must not harm deliverability (no heavy imagery; keep text-forward).

Goals / success criteria
- Mobile: no horizontal scroll at 320px; comfortable line length; CTA visible above the fold on iPhone 13/SE.
- Copy fit: supports long subject lines and longer code snippets without breaking layout.
- Accessibility: color contrast, readable typography, link/CTA affordance, meaningful preheader.
- Operational: easy for Marketing Ops to swap modules (hero, bullets, code block, CTA, footer) without hand-editing fragile tables.

Non-goals
- Major brand refresh or new illustration system.
- Rewriting the sequence copy (owned in PM-659 / copy doc); we’ll provide fit feedback + suggested trims.

Deliverables
1) Figma spec for the v2 email template: desktop + mobile layout, typography scale, spacing, component states.
2) Final HTML module guidance (what’s safe across clients; do/don’t list).
3) Copy-fit recommendations: max character counts for subject, headline, subhead, CTA.
4) QA screenshots across major clients (Litmus pass list).

Links
- Confluence design spec (email v2): sources/confluence/product-docs/design-specs/email-onboarding-drip-v2.json
- Messaging guidelines: sources/confluence/sales-enablement/positioning-and-messaging/onboarding-messaging-guidelines-hosted-api.json
- Draft copy variants: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/onboarding-email-copy-variants-v2.json
- Send schedule/triggers: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/onboarding-email-send-schedule-and-triggers.json

title: In-console onboarding nudges — experiment design (targeting, holdout, measurement)
preview:
## Context
Part of the **Lifecycle Growth Experiments Sprint (Hosted API)**. This ticket defines the experiment design for **in-console onboarding nudges** (targeting, holdout, variants, and measurement) so we can ship Week 2 without changing the core product architecture.

Primary sprint goal: lift activation (% reaching **first successful request** + **first 100k tokens** within 7 days) and reduce time-to-value for new developers.

## Hypotheses
H1: Contextual nudges shown at high-intent surfaces in the Console will increase completion of key onboarding steps (API key creation, first request) and improve downstream activation within 7 days.

H2: For “Send your first request,” an in-console, copyable snippet or prefilled Playground flow will outperform docs deep-links on step completion and time-to-first-token.

## Nudge inventory (v1 in this sprint)
We will evaluate a small set to keep the experiment interpretable.

N1 — Create your API key
- Intended step: **api_key_created**
- Eligible state: S1 (no API key)
- Primary surface: Home/Project Overview; also API Keys page

N2 — Send your first request (variant test)
- Intended step: **first_successful_request**
- Eligible state: S2 (API key exists, no successful request)
- Primary surface: Home/Project Overview; optionally Playground

N3 — Enable streaming (recommended)
- Intended step: first streaming request (secondary, not a primary activation KPI)
- Eligible state: S3 (first request done, streaming not used)

N4 — View usage & costs
- Intended step: usage dashboard visited (secondary / retention proxy)
- Eligible state: S3+ and has not visited Usage

Note: We will only render **one** onboarding nudge at a time by priority: N1 → N2 → N4 → N3.

## Targeting & eligibility
### Base eligibility
- User is within **7 days of signup** OR has not reached first_successful_request.
- Hosted API console experience (exclude Private-only tenants where flows differ).

### Exclusions
- Users already in **S4 (≥100k tokens)**: suppress onboarding nudges.
- If we have a reliable suppression signal: suppress for accounts flagged as **CS-led / active enterprise onboarding** to avoid conflicting flows.
- Internal staff/dogfood are excluded by default; enable via separate internal-only flag for QA.

### Placement rules (v1)
Allowed placements this sprint:
- Console Home / Project Overview (primary)
- API Keys page (contextual)
- Playground (only for N2 variant C, if enabled)

Frequency / annoyance constraints:
- No modals.
- Do not show more than 1 nudge per page view.
- Do not repeat the same nudge >3 sessions unless onboarding state changes.
- Dismissal persistence:
  - “Not now” = snooze 72 hours
  - “Don’t show again” = permanent dismiss until step completion/state change

## Holdout & experiment structure
### Framework test (primary)
- 10% **holdout**: no nudges render anywhere.
- 90% **treatment**: nudges enabled.
- Assignment: sticky at **user_id**, duration 30 days.

### Variant test (within treatment)
N2 variant split (within treatment only):
- Variant A (33%): “Copy quickstart snippet” → inline code drawer
- Variant B (33%): “Open quickstart” → docs deep-link
- Variant C (34%): “Try in Playground” → prefilled playground

Notes:
- We will keep N1/N3/N4 copy constant for Week 2 to limit confounds.
- Variant analysis will focus on step completion and time-to-first-successful-request for S2 users.

## Metrics & measurement plan
### Primary outcomes (experiment-level)
1) Activation within 7 days of signup:
- % reaching **first_successful_request**
- % reaching **token_milestone_reached (100k)**

2) Time-to-value:
- time from signup → first_successful_request (TTFT proxy, in minutes/hours)

### Step-level metrics (per nudge)
- Step completion rate within 7 days of first eligible exposure:
  - N1: api_key_created
  - N2: first_successful_request
  - N4: usage dashboard viewed
  - N3: streaming request observed

### Interaction metrics
- CTR: console_onboarding_nudge_cta_clicked / console_onboarding_nudge_rendered
- Dismiss/snooze rate

### Guardrails
- No increase in API key creation failures or blocked UI states.
- No increase in onboarding-related support volume attributable to nudges.
- Dismiss rates: watch for “permanent dismiss” >25% as an annoyance signal.

## Telemetry requirements (eventing)
Nudge events (required):
- console_onboarding_nudge_rendered
  - props: nudge_id, placement, cohort (holdout|treatment), variant, user_age_days, org_id, project_id
- console_onboarding_nudge_cta_clicked
  - props: nudge_id, placement, cohort, variant, cta_id
- console_onboarding_nudge_dismissed
  - props: nudge_id, placement, dismiss_type (snooze|permanent)

Activation events (dependency):
- api_key_created
- first_successful_request
- token_milestone_reached (milestone=100k)

Dedupe expectations:
- Render: once per page view.
- CTA clicks: once per click.
- Activation milestones: idempotent/deduped per lifecycle spec (see ENG-10492).

## Analysis approach / reporting
- Minimum run time: 7 days after 90% rollout (maintain 10% holdout).
- Report absolute + relative lift vs holdout; include confidence intervals where feasible.
- Segment cuts (non-blocking): region, REST vs SDK (if we can infer), company size proxy, new vs returning within 7 days.

Data quality checks:
- Validate holdout has ~0 nudge_rendered events.
- Validate event volume stability after rollout ramps.
- Spot-check for duplicate signup/activation emissions (known risk; track against recent dedupe fixes).

## Launch plan (Week 2)
1) Internal-only dogfood: 2 days
2) 5% external: 1 day
3) 25% external: 2 days
4) 90% external + 10% holdout: remainder of sprint

Kill switch: lgx_console_nudges_enabled OFF if:
- Nudge blocks API key creation/first request flows
- Significant error spike or support escalation

## Acceptance criteria
- Holdout vs treatment cohort assignment is sticky and visible in events.
- Nudge render/click/dismiss events are emitted with required props.
- No more than one nudge appears per page view; dismissal persists across sessions.
- Dashboard can answer: activation lift vs holdout; step completion lift per nudge; CTR/dismiss rates.

## Open questions (tracked here; resolved for Week 2 scope unless noted)
1) Checklist vs single-card nudge on Home?
- Decision (Week 2): single-card nudge only. Checklist can be explored post-sprint.

2) “First request” default CTA?
- Decision: run A/B/C in parallel within treatment.

3) Enterprise/CS-led suppression signal availability?
- Decision: do not block launch on suppression. If signal not reliable, we won’t suppress to avoid false exclusions; we’ll instead add a manual exclusion list later if needed.

## Review notes / comments
2026-01-18 — Kenji Nakamura (Design):
- “Prefer we avoid a ‘marketing’ tone; keep titles action-oriented. Keep nudge height small so it doesn’t push primary page content below the fold on laptop.”
- “Make dismissal UI obvious + accessible (keyboard focus on dismiss).”

2026-01-19 — Dylan Brooks (Eng):
- “Holdout must be enforced server-side (or at least by flag service) to guarantee ‘no render’ and avoid client bugs leaking exposure.”
- “We should prioritize getting render/click/dismiss events correct over adding extra placements. Home + API Keys page is enough for Week 2.”

2026-01-20 — Paula Mendes (Telemetry):
- “Please ensure nudge events include cohort + variant. Also include project_id; we’ve seen users with multiple projects skew step completion if we only look at org_id.”
- “For ‘first_successful_request,’ confirm dedupe rules (ENG-10492) to avoid double-counting retries.”

2026-01-21 — Ben Carter (Growth):
- “OK with 10% holdout; we need it stable through end of sprint for credible readout.”
- “For Week 2 readout: prioritize (1) first_successful_request lift, (2) TTFT proxy, (3) dismissal rates.”

## Next steps
- Engineering: implement cohort assignment + holdout (ENG-10501) and eventing (ENG-10492).
- Design: finalize copy + CTA labels for N1/N2/N3/N4 and confirm placement spacing.
- Analytics/Obs: ensure activation funnel dashboard includes cohort breakdown and nudge interaction panels (ENG-10533).

title: Onboarding email drip v2 — experiment design (variants, targeting, and readout plan)
preview:
Owner: Ben Carter (Growth)
PM partner: Paula Mendes (telemetry/rollouts input)
Marketing ops: Tyler Benson (send tooling + attribution)
Messaging review: Soojin Lee
Design: Tessa Morgan (template), Kenji Nakamura (minor layout/CTA polish)
Compliance: Michael Grant (CAN-SPAM), Olga Petrov (GDPR)

Context
We’re running a v2 of the Hosted API onboarding drip to reduce time-to-first-value and lift activation within 7 days. This ticket defines the experiment design for the email track in the Lifecycle Growth Experiments Sprint.

Primary hypothesis
A shorter, more “do the first request now” onboarding sequence (with clearer snippet + fewer competing links) will increase:
- % of new signups reaching “first successful request” within 7 days
- % of new signups reaching “first 100k tokens” within 7 days
and will reduce median time-to-first-request.

Experiment type
A/B + holdout, randomized at signup via lifecycle cohort assignment.

Cohorts / assignment
- Holdout (no lifecycle onboarding emails; transactional emails still allowed): 10%
- Control (existing v1 sequence): 45%
- Treatment (v2 sequence): 45%

Notes:
- Assignment key: user_id at signup.
- Cohort must persist across devices/sessions.
- If a user is in holdout/control/treatment, they stay there for the entire 7-day onboarding window.

Eligibility (who gets assigned)
Include:
- New self-serve Hosted API signups (console signup) with verified email.
- First-time users only (no prior signup in past 180 days by email hash).

Exclude / suppress:
- Users who have opted out of marketing/lifecycle emails (respect preferences + global unsub).
- Internal domains + test accounts (redwoodinference.com, redwood.ai, etc.).
- Accounts associated with active enterprise opportunities or sensitive accounts per Sales exclusions (domain-level suppression list maintained weekly).
- Regulated/limited-tracking segments where policy disallows lifecycle targeting beyond essential comms (per data-handling note).

Segmentation (messaging logic)
Within each cohort, emails are triggered based on activation state. We’re explicitly optimizing for the “stalled before first request” segment.

State definitions (event-driven)
- Signed up: event=signup_completed
- API key created: event=api_key_created
- First successful request: event=hosted_api_first_request_success
- 100k token milestone: event=tokens_100k_within_7d

Treatment (v2) email sequence
All emails use Redwood messaging guidelines: developer-first, performance + reliability proof points, minimal fluff. Tone: helpful, direct.

Email 0 — “Welcome / create your API key”
- Trigger: signup_completed
- Delay: ~5 minutes (allow verification + initial console exploration)
- Goal: drive API key creation
- Primary CTA: “Create API key” (deep-link to console)
- Secondary CTA: “Hello world: first request” (docs/quickstart)

Email 1 — “Run your first request (copy/paste)”
- Trigger: signup_completed AND NOT hosted_api_first_request_success
- Delay: 2 hours
- Goal: get first request; reduce time-to-first-token
- Primary CTA: “Copy snippet” / “Run first request” (landing page with snippet aligned to quickstart)
- Include: short troubleshooting section (missing key, wrong base URL, model name)

Email 2 — “If you’re stuck, here are 3 common fixes”
- Trigger: signup_completed AND NOT hosted_api_first_request_success
- Delay: 24 hours
- Goal: unblock common failure modes; reduce support load
- Primary CTA: “Debug checklist”
- Secondary CTA: “Open support ticket” / “Join office hours” (guardrail: keep this lightweight)

Email 3 — “Next step: streaming + cost/latency tips”
- Trigger: hosted_api_first_request_success AND NOT tokens_100k_within_7d
- Delay: 48 hours after first_request_success
- Goal: convert first success into real usage; drive streaming enablement + dashboard visit
- Primary CTA: “Enable streaming” (docs)
- Secondary CTA: “View usage dashboard” (console deep-link)

Email 4 — “Ship something small this week (template)”
- Trigger: hosted_api_first_request_success AND NOT tokens_100k_within_7d
- Delay: Day 6 after signup
- Goal: encourage a small build (example app/template) and return visit
- Primary CTA: “Minimal chat app template”

- Keep existing onboarding sequence as-is (current copy + timing).

We’ll run a lightweight subject line split for Email 1 to improve opens without changing downstream content.
- Variant A: “Your first request in 60 seconds”
- Variant B: “Copy/paste this to call Redwood (first request)”

- utm_campaign=onboarding_v2
- utm_content={email_0|email_1|email_2|email_3|email_4}

1) Activation-1: % of new signups with hosted_api_first_request_success within 7 days (primary)
2) Activation-2: % of new signups reaching tokens_100k_within_7d (co-primary)

Secondary metrics:
- Median time-to-first-request (signup_completed -> hosted_api_first_request_success)
- Email-to-activation assisted rate (click within 24h preceding first_success)
- D7 active usage (any successful request on day 7)
- Unsubscribe rate per send + overall
- Complaint rate (spam complaints) and hard bounce rate

Guardrails / stop conditions
Deliverability & compliance thresholds (per send and rolling 7d):
- Hard bounce rate: < 2.0%
- Spam complaint rate: < 0.08%
- Unsubscribe rate: < 1.5% per email (investigate if exceeded; pause if sustained)
Product/support guardrails:
- Support ticket volume tagged “onboarding email” should not increase > 20% WoW without clear root cause.
Experiment integrity:
- If we detect event tracking gaps by region or duplicate emission (funnel mismatch > 3% vs baseline), pause analysis and fix instrumentation before calling a winner.

Analysis plan
- Funnel dashboard: signup -> api_key_created -> first_request_success -> tokens_100k_within_7d

- Compare holdout vs control to estimate baseline activation without lifecycle emails (sanity check + future budget justification).

Sample size / duration
- Minimum run: 7 days of exposure + 7-day conversion window (so 14 days total before final call), unless we hit guardrails.
- Interim readouts: weekly (Week 1 + Week 2), with directional callouts but no “winner” declaration until conversion window closes.

Copy & design
- [ ] Soojin Lee approves copy (messaging + claims)
- [ ] DES-311 email template refresh merged (mobile, typography, dark mode sanity)
- [ ] Links checked (console deep-links, quickstart snippet, streaming docs)

Ops & compliance
- [ ] Unsubscribe link present + functional for all variants
- [ ] Preference center respected across lifecycle sends (ENG-10518)
- [ ] Suppression lists synced (internal/test + active opp domains)
- [ ] Send ramp: start at 10% of eligible traffic for 24h, then 50%, then 100% if deliverability stable

Instrumentation
- [ ] Cohort assignment persisted and included as user property (control/treatment/holdout)
- [ ] Email events flowing: delivered/open/click (with message_id)
- [ ] Product events deduped and validated (ENG-10492)
- [ ] Dashboard panels live + alert for sudden drop in activation (ENG-10533)

QA
- [ ] End-to-end dry run in staging with test accounts in each cohort
- [ ] Verify trigger logic: state-based sends stop when user activates (no “you’re stuck” emails after first success)
- [ ] Verify regional send timing and time zones (avoid sending overnight local time for EU)

Readout plan
Use the standard experiment readout template. Weekly readout must include:
- Exposure counts by cohort (holdout/control/treatment)
- Deliverability metrics and any throttling actions
- Primary funnel metrics + lift
- Segment notes (where treatment helped/hurt)
- Decision: keep/iterate/kill + exact next step (e.g., roll v2 to 100% or run copy iteration)

Open questions (resolved in comments)
- Whether Email 3 should be keyed off “first request success” vs “api key created” (leaning first request).
- How aggressive we are on deep-linking into console vs pushing a docs landing page (tradeoff: friction vs attribution clarity).

Definition of Done
- Experiment launched with cohorts in production and ramp plan executed.
- Dashboards populated and validated.
- Week 1 readout produced with initial findings + next-step recommendation.

title: Anchor-based contrast suite and ranked-pair concordance eval for embedding+reranker
preview: Build an offline evaluation harness that ties config changes and model swaps to concrete embedding + reranker quality signals. The suite will: (1) construct anchor queries from production cohorts, (2) generate hard negatives and synthetic paraphrase negatives, (3) run the embedding + reranker pipeline to produce ranked lists, and (4) compute a small, interpretable metric set including recall@K, precision@K, MRR, ranked-pair concordance, and embedding-space shift scores. The goal is to produce a delta report for every config/model change that can be used to gate rollouts and trigger automated investigations. The harness should be fast enough for nightly pre-merge runs on sampled cohorts and robust enough to run as part of CI for significant config PRs.

title: Post-pilot readout + next-steps plan — Vertical ABM (Regulated Workloads: FinServ + Healthcare)
preview:
Goal
Create a durable post-pilot readout covering (1) pipeline outcomes, (2) lessons learned / what changed in messaging + process, (3) what assets converted, and (4) a proposal to scale ABM to additional regulated segments without pulling in major product scope.

Audience
Exec staff (Marketing + Sales + Product), Security/GRC, SE leadership. This is intended to become the “v1 playbook” artifact for repeatable regulated outbound.

Pilot snapshot
- Timebox: ~8 weeks (prep + execution + close) May–June 2025
- Verticals: Financial services (primary) + Healthcare/PHI-adjacent (secondary)
- Primary products: Redwood Dedicated + Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem)
- Core buyer set: CISO/Security, Platform/ML Eng, Procurement

Success criteria (from brief)
- Validate repeatable positioning + outbound motion for compliance-driven buyers
- Produce a baseline set of vertical assets (landing page, one-pagers, security FAQ/evidence pack index, talk tracks, sequences)
- Generate qualified pipeline (meetings, SQLs, pilots/POCs, conversion to procurement)

Outcomes (high-level)
Pipeline / meetings
- Target accounts: 38 total (FinServ: 24, Healthcare: 14)
- Coverage: 32/38 had a named owner + first touch within first 10 business days of execution
- Positive replies: 21 accounts (55% of targeted accounts) had at least one positive signal (reply, referral, security questionnaire request, or “send materials”) 
- Meetings booked: 14 (FinServ: 9, Healthcare: 5)
- SQLs created: 6 (FinServ: 4, Healthcare: 2)
- POCs started: 2 (Northbridge Banking, Helix Health System)
- Procurement/legal engaged: 1 (Trident Financial Services)

Qualitative signals
- “Regulated-ready” posture consistently drove the first conversion event (security call / evidence request), not performance claims alone.
- The best-performing hook was: predictable throughput + compliance controls + deployment flexibility (VPC/on-prem) framed as “avoid building and operating inference infrastructure under audit constraints.”
- Most late-stage risk was not product gaps; it was response-time + consistency on security questionnaires and commercial packaging clarity (Dedicated vs Private vs hosted, plus retention/residency caveats).

Account outcomes (not exhaustive; focus on stage movement)
- Northbridge Banking (FinServ): POC complete; success criteria met on latency + logging requirements; next step is a rollout plan + procurement discovery (Dedicated vs Private decision leaning Private/VPC due to internal network policy).
- Helix Health System (Healthcare): POC scoped + executed; outcome was “possible” but additional PHI guardrails and clearer retention/residency controls needed for full go-live; next step is security follow-up + procurement timing alignment.
- Brightline Insurance (FinServ): security review completed; evidence pack requested; next step is a short technical deep dive (audit log export format + SAML/SCIM) before committing to POC.
- Regal Wealth Management (FinServ): technical deep dive completed on audit logs + KMS/HSM; strong fit signal; waiting on internal platform team bandwidth to scope pilot.
- Trident Financial Services (FinServ): progressed to procurement/legal; redlines in flight (DPA/SLA + residency language); outcome TBD.

What we shipped (assets + enablement)
Assets produced/updated during the pilot (v1)
- Regulated workloads landing page (with cross-links to Security FAQ + evidence pack index)
- FinServ messaging framework v1
- Healthcare messaging framework v1
- Regulated workloads security FAQ (sales-ready)
- Evidence pack index page (request/sharing guidance)
- Regulated Workloads ABM deck v1
- Regulated inference competitive battlecard
- Outbound email sequence (FinServ) + LinkedIn ads creative brief
- Webinar outline + follow-up templates (webinar not executed within pilot window; outline used for 1:1 talk track)

Asset conversion (directional; pull exact counts from dashboard spec)
- Highest-converting assets in “first conversion event” (meeting / security call):
  1) Security FAQ (link sent in outbound follow-up) 
  2) Evidence pack index (created confidence + made the request process concrete)
  3) ABM deck (used in live calls; reduced time to “next step”)
- Landing page performance was “good enough” for ABM but not yet a scalable demand gen engine; the CTA that worked best was “Book a security + architecture review” over “Download benchmark.”

Messaging learnings (what worked / what we changed)
What worked
- Lead with auditability + control: audit logs, encryption/KMS, SSO/SAML, data handling boundaries.
- Pair compliance with economics: “predictable throughput and cost controls under real traffic,” not generic “fast GPUs.”
- Emphasize deployment flexibility as a risk reducer: Hosted → Dedicated → Private/VPC/on-prem as they mature.

What did not work (or triggered friction)
- “HIPAA-ready” language in healthcare; too loaded without strict qualification and legal review. Replaced with “PHI-adjacent workflows” + “supports common security controls; details depend on deployment and customer configuration.”
- Overly model-centric positioning; buyers cared more about operational posture (logs, access controls, change management, retention).

Top objections and effective responses
- “We need on-prem / no public internet access” → Redwood Private deployment pattern + customer-managed networking; SE-led architecture review.
- “We need audit logs exported to our SIEM” → show log fields + export options; clarify timeline for any format specifics; point to examples.
- “We need strict data retention controls” → clarify current retention policy per deployment and what is customer-configurable; align on requirements early.
- “We already have GPUs” → frame Redwood as the inference runtime + control plane + operational tooling (rollouts, fallback, observability) and faster path to SLA compliance.

Process learnings (internal)
What went well
- Weekly governance with Sales/PMM/Security/SE prevented major claim drift.
- Evidence pack “index + request process” reduced ad-hoc doc hunting and lowered security review cycle time.

Bottlenecks
- Security questionnaire turnaround was inconsistent when asks landed via multiple channels (email + Slack + calls). We need a single intake and SLA.
- Packaging confusion on Dedicated vs Private (especially for VPC-private endpoints and data boundaries). We need a one-page internal decision tree and a consistent external explanation.

Product/docs feedback surfaced (non-scope, but high leverage)
- SAML metadata validation edge cases surfaced during pilot; document known constraints + recommended IdP configs.
- Audit log export formats and field-level definitions need a single canonical reference (docs + example snippet), especially for SIEM ingestion.
- Retention/residency language needs a standardized “what’s supported today vs by contract vs by configuration” section.

Proposed next steps (scale plan)
Recommendation: double down on FinServ first; keep Healthcare as a smaller, tightly-qualified motion.

Why FinServ first
- Faster path to “security call → POC” when framed as auditability + latency + predictable throughput.
- Higher incidence of Dedicated/Private fit signals (VPC, SIEM, KMS/HSM) without the additional PHI-specific program requirements.

90-day plan (v2 ABM)
1) Tighten the motion
- Create a single “Regulated Intake” channel + lightweight form for security questionnaires and evidence requests; commit to internal SLA (e.g., acknowledge in 1 business day, first pass in 3 business days).
- Publish an internal “Dedicated vs Private” decision tree + externally-safe explainer (no over-claims).

2) Refresh assets (minimum viable)
- Update landing page with 2 clearer architecture patterns (Dedicated in isolated environment vs Private VPC/on-prem).
- Add a security review prep checklist (what we’ll cover; what to bring) to reduce back-and-forth.
- Add a public-friendly proof point section (genericized benchmarks + operational outcomes). If no named customer logos, use anonymized “reference profile” format with Security-approved wording.

3) Expand targeting channels
- Add AWS Marketplace co-sell motions for accounts already in procurement processes; standardize private offer steps and who owns each step.
- Run 1 webinar or virtual roundtable only after the v2 landing page + proof point section is ready (avoid scaling top-of-funnel before mid-funnel is de-risked).

4) Measurement upgrades
- Enforce per-account “first conversion event” tracking (reply vs meeting vs evidence request vs security call) so we don’t over-credit meetings.
- Track cycle time metrics: first touch → first meeting, first meeting → security review, security review → POC start.

Decision points
- By end of next 2-week sprint: confirm v2 scope, owners, and publish the internal packaging decision tree.
- By day 45: decide whether Healthcare expands beyond “PHI-adjacent only” based on whether we can standardize retention/residency language and reduce legal review overhead.

Open questions / asks
- Security: confirm the minimum externally-shareable statements for retention/residency by deployment type; add to FAQ.
- Product (Private): confirm roadmap stance on audit export formats (what’s supported today vs planned).
- Sales leadership: confirm whether we want to gate “evidence pack” behind a meeting for ABM (improves qualification) or keep as request-based (improves conversion rate).

- Lead with auditability + control: audit logs, encryption/KMS, SSO/SAML, data handling boundaries.
- Pair compliance with economics: “predictable throughput and cost controls under real traffic,” not generic “fast GPUs.”
- Emphasize deployment flexibility as a risk reducer: Hosted → Dedicated → Private/VPC/on-prem as they mature.

What did not work (or triggered friction)
- “HIPAA-ready” language in healthcare; too loaded without strict qualification and legal review. Replaced with “PHI-adjacent workflows” + “supports common security controls; details depend on deployment and customer configuration.”
- Overly model-centric positioning; buyers cared more about operational posture (logs, access controls, change management, retention).

Top objections and effective responses
- “We need on-prem / no public internet access” → Redwood Private deployment pattern + customer-managed networking; SE-led architecture review.
- “We need audit logs exported to our SIEM” → show log fields + export options; clarify timeline for any format specifics; point to examples.
- “We need strict data retention controls” → clarify current retention policy per deployment and what is customer-configurable; align on requirements early.
- “We already have GPUs” → frame Redwood as the inference runtime + control plane + operational tooling (rollouts, fallback, observability) and faster path to SLA compliance.

Process learnings (internal)
What went well
- Weekly governance with Sales/PMM/Security/SE prevented major claim drift.
- Evidence pack “index + request process” reduced ad-hoc doc hunting and lowered security review cycle time.

Bottlenecks
- Security questionnaire turnaround was inconsistent when asks landed via multiple channels (email + Slack + calls). We need a single intake and SLA.
- Packaging confusion on Dedicated vs Private (especially for VPC-private endpoints and data boundaries). We need a one-page internal decision tree and a consistent external explanation.

Product/docs feedback surfaced (non-scope, but high leverage)
- SAML metadata validation edge cases surfaced during pilot; document known constraints + recommended IdP configs.
- Audit log export formats and field-level definitions need a single canonical reference (docs + example snippet), especially for SIEM ingestion.
- Retention/residency language needs a standardized “what’s supported today vs by contract vs by configuration” section.

Proposed next steps (scale plan)
Recommendation: double down on FinServ first; keep Healthcare as a smaller, tightly-qualified motion.

Why FinServ first
- Faster path to “security call → POC” when framed as auditability + latency + predictable throughput.
- Higher incidence of Dedicated/Private fit signals (VPC, SIEM, KMS/HSM) without the additional PHI-specific program requirements.

90-day plan (v2 ABM)
1) Tighten the motion
- Create a single “Regulated Intake” channel + lightweight form for security questionnaires and evidence requests; commit to internal SLA (e.g., acknowledge in 1 business day, first pass in 3 business days).
- Publish an internal “Dedicated vs Private” decision tree + externally-safe explainer (no over-claims).

2) Refresh assets (minimum viable)
- Update landing page with 2 clearer architecture patterns (Dedicated in isolated environment vs Private VPC/on-prem).
- Add a security review prep checklist (what we’ll cover; what to bring) to reduce back-and-forth.
- Add a public-friendly proof point section (genericized benchmarks + operational outcomes). If no named customer logos, use anonymized “reference profile” format with Security-approved wording.

3) Expand targeting channels
- Add AWS Marketplace co-sell motions for accounts already in procurement processes; standardize private offer steps and who owns each step.
- Run 1 webinar or virtual roundtable only after the v2 landing page + proof point section is ready (avoid scaling top-of-funnel before mid-funnel is de-risked).

4) Measurement upgrades
- Enforce per-account “first conversion event” tracking (reply vs meeting vs evidence request vs security call) so we don’t over-credit meetings.
- Track cycle time metrics: first touch → first meeting, first meeting → security review, security review → POC start.

Decision points
- By end of next 2-week sprint: confirm v2 scope, owners, and publish the internal packaging decision tree.
- By day 45: decide whether Healthcare expands beyond “PHI-adjacent only” based on whether we can standardize retention/residency language and reduce legal review overhead.

Open questions / asks
- Security: confirm the minimum externally-shareable statements for retention/residency by deployment type; add to FAQ.
- Product (Private): confirm roadmap stance on audit export formats (what’s supported today vs planned).
- Sales leadership: confirm whether we want to gate “evidence pack” behind a meeting for ABM (improves qualification) or keep as request-based (improves conversion rate).

title: Vertical ABM pilot plan + KPIs (regulated workloads)
preview:
Goal
Run a 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot focused on regulated workloads (initial verticals: Financial Services + Healthcare) to validate repeatable positioning/outbound motion for security/compliance-driven buyers and generate qualified pipeline for Redwood Dedicated + Redwood Private.

In scope
- Target account selection + assignment (NA + EMEA)
- Persona map + messaging alignment for: CISO/Security, Platform/ML Eng, Procurement
- Vertical assets (landing page, one-pagers, security FAQ + evidence pack index, talk tracks, sequences)
- Outreach + meeting booking + discovery/demo/security reviews
- Optional short pilots (benchmark + architecture validation) with SE support
- Measurement: coverage, engagement, meetings, SQLs, POC starts, stage velocity, closed-won signals

Out of scope
- Major new product features (OK: minor docs updates/clarifications + enablement)

Primary deliverables (this ticket tracks plan + KPI definitions + weekly execution status)
1) Targeting
- Account list for FinServ + Healthcare, with score + intent signals + owner
- Persona mapping per account (where known)

2) Assets
- Regulated workloads landing page
- Vertical one-pagers (FinServ Private/Dedicated + Healthcare PHI-adjacent)
- Security FAQ for regulated buyers + evidence pack index + sharing guidance
- Outbound email sequence(s) + LinkedIn ad brief
- Sales enablement deck + competitive battlecard alignment

3) Measurement / ops
- Weekly operating cadence + risk log
- KPI dashboard spec + data sources (HubSpot, Gmail, LinkedIn)
- UTM conventions + attribution approach

Working links
- Week-by-week plan (Drive): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/abm-pilot-week-by-week-plan.json
- Target accounts (Drive): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/target-account-list-finserv-health-sheet.json

- Landing page copy draft (Drive): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/landing-page-copy-draft-regulated.json
- Metrics dashboard spec (Drive): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/abm-pilot-metrics-dashboard-spec.json

Owners / RACI
- Pilot execution + reporting: Ben Carter
- Executive sponsor / approvals: Camila Reyes
- Messaging + positioning / claim approvals: Soojin Lee (with Dr. Aisha Rahman + Naomi Feldman for security/compliance review)
- Marketing ops + attribution / tracking: Tyler Benson
- CRM hygiene + pipeline analytics: Isabelle Laurent
- Outbound program: Grace O'Connor + Rohan Mehta
- Sales coverage: Stephanie Nguyen (NA), Lukas Meyer (EMEA); AEs: Avery Johnson, Priyom Das, Hannah Schmitt, Diego Fernandez
- SE coverage for technical deep dives/pilots: Markus Klein (lead), Fatima Noor (regulated lead)

Timeline (baseline)
- Weeks 1–2 (Prep): finalize vertical choice + ICP, build account list, finalize messaging, produce/QA assets, finalize measurement
- Weeks 3–6 (Execute): outreach, meetings, security reviews, 1–2 pilots (as qualified)
- Weeks 7–8 (Close): convert to next stage, procurement support, post-pilot readout + plan

KPI definitions (pilot success criteria)
Top-level targets (pilot-level)
- Target account coverage: 60–80 named accounts with assigned owner + next step recorded
- Meetings booked: 12–18 total (mix of security/discovery/technical deep dives)
- SQLs created: 6–10 (explicit project, timeline, deployment path, champion)
- POCs/pilots launched: 2–4 (benchmark + architecture validation); at least 1 in FinServ OR healthcare reaching closeout
- Pipeline created: tracked as $ qualified pipeline for Dedicated/Private (report range weekly; do not commit publicly)
- Stage velocity: time from first meeting → security review → pilot scoping (trend over time)

Leading indicators (weekly)
- Outreach volume and reply rate (by persona)
- Landing page engagement (CTR, form fills, meeting-request conversion)
- Evidence pack requests and security review progression (questionnaires opened, evidence shared, follow-up scheduled)
- Partner/co-sell sourced intros (AWS Marketplace motion) and conversion

Quality gates (compliance-safe marketing)
- Claims must match approved language from Security/PMM threads (avoid “HIPAA-certified/ready” style over-claims; use precise phrasing and footnotes)
- Evidence pack sharing per confidentiality guidance; log who has access

Risks / mitigations
- Security review bandwidth bottleneck → mitigate with evidence pack index + pre-baked FAQ answers + weekly 30-min Security office hours
- Ambiguous packaging for Dedicated vs Private → mitigate with internal guidance + consistent talk track + escalation path (Lauren Bishop / Vivek Kulkarni)
- Low-volume LinkedIn audiences in EMEA → mitigate with account-based outreach + partner co-sell + tighter job-title filters

Acceptance criteria
- KPI dashboard spec finalized and implemented in tracking sheet (owner: Tyler/Isabelle)
- Target account list v1 shipped + owners assigned + status hygiene process documented
- Landing page + one-pagers published with Security-approved language
- Email sequence in-market + A/B variant noted
- Weekly standup cadence established with updates captured below
- Post-pilot readout ticket (PM-1440) kicked off with inputs ready
