title: Sales enablement playbook: review, approve, and publish PLG signal follow-up v1
preview:
## Context
We’re rolling out PLG-to-sales signal routing (Hosted self-serve → sales-assisted upgrades to Dedicated/Private). This ticket covers the final review + approvals for the v1 follow-up playbook, incorporating AE/SE/CS feedback from the NA pilot and publishing the final version in Confluence.

This playbook is the “single source of truth” for:
- which PLG signals are sales-worthy (threshold + intent)
- what we say (messaging) and how we sequence it (cadence)
- who owns the first touch (AE vs CSM vs SE) and when to loop in SE/security
- what outcomes must be logged for attribution (meeting booked, eval started, opp created, disqualified)

## Owner(s)
- DRI (Playbook): Ben Carter
- PM approver: Nikhil Sharma
- Sales approver: Stephanie Nguyen
- SE approver: Markus Klein
- CS alignment: Jasmine Liu
- CRM/Ops check: David Kim + Isabelle Laurent

## Deliverable
- Publish **PLG Signal Follow-up Playbook v1** in Confluence under Sales Enablement.
- Announce in #general and #sales with: what’s changing, pilot scope, where to give feedback.
- Run 45-min enablement/training for NA pilot AEs + SDRs + SEs.

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] Playbook includes final list of v1 signals + definitions (aligned with schema v1 + suppression rules)
- [x] Messaging sections cover top 3 motions: **Scale**, **Reliability/SLO pain**, **Security/Compliance**
- [x] Clear routing/ownership guidance (preserve existing owner; CSM loop-in rules)
- [x] Cadence v1 included with exit criteria + “do not contact” guardrails
- [x] SE involvement triggers are explicit (what requires immediate SE assignment)
- [x] Pricing guidance link + guardrails included (no unapproved discounts; when to use credits)
- [x] Security FAQ included + when to loop security
- [x] Outcome logging instructions included (HubSpot properties + minimum required fields)
- [x] Training session checklist complete; recording + slides posted

## Content edits required (v1)
### 1) Signals → recommended action mapping
Incorporate pilot feedback: AEs want fewer “FYI” signals and more “actionable” bundles.
- Consolidate into 4 play types:
  1. **Throughput/scale** (sustained spend velocity, sustained token volume, new workspaces/team expansion)
  2. **Reliability pain** (repeated 429s, elevated 5xx / latency SLO misses)
  3. **Dedicated intent** (Dedicated docs, pricing clicks, “capacity” FAQ views)
  4. **Private/compliance intent** (VPC/on-prem docs, security FAQ, audit log/retention/KMS pages)
- Add a short “What NOT to do” box: do not over-interpret single doc views; avoid outreach during active support escalations.

### 2) Cadence v1 polish (tone + timing)
Update cadence copy to address AE feedback from #sales:
- Reduce “hard sell” tone in first touch; open with value: predictability (latency/throughput), cost controls, and reliability.
- Explicit rule: if signal is **Reliability pain**, offer SE benchmark help within 48h.
- Add “one-thread rule”: keep email thread per account; do not start parallel sequences from multiple reps.

### 3) SE + CS handoff rules (make it unambiguous)
- If existing paying customer w/ assigned CSM: CSM is notified same day; AE coordinates before outreach.
- If Private/compliance intent: auto-create SE task + optionally loop security reviewer (Aisha/Marcus) if they request evidence.
- Add a decision tree:
  - New self-serve + threshold reached → AE owns
  - Existing customer + expansion signal → AE + CSM coordinate; CSM may lead intro depending on relationship
  - Security/compliance intent → AE owns outreach; SE runs technical/security session; security reviewer as needed

### 4) Outcome tracking requirements
Add a minimal required set of outcomes to log in HubSpot for attribution:
- Outcome field options: Contacted, Meeting Booked, Disqualified (Noise/No fit), Customer Escalation (route to Support/CS), Evaluation Started, Opp Created.
- Required note template for “Disqualified (Noise)” to help tuning suppression rules.

## Training session plan (45 minutes)
**Audience:** NA pilot AEs, SDRs (if used), SEs, CS observers

**Agenda**
1) Why we’re doing PLG signals (5 min)
2) What signals you’ll see + how they’re scored/suppressed (10 min)
3) How to act: play types + cadence (15 min)
4) Live walkthrough: HubSpot company view + tasks + logging outcomes (10 min)
5) Q&A + feedback loop (5 min)

**Training checklist**
- [x] 5-slide deck (problem → signals → plays → HubSpot workflow → outcomes)
- [x] Demo account screenshots (HelioSearch + Quartz false positive)
- [x] “SE escalation snippets” added (benchmark offer, Dedicated sizing questions)
- [x] Recording + slides posted to Confluence
- [x] Follow-up message posted in #sales with links + where to ask questions

## Risks / open questions
- Noise: playbook must reinforce suppression windows and “don’t spam” rule (esp. doc-intent-only signals).
- Conflicts with marketing lifecycle emails: need explicit note to check whether marketing nurture is active (see Marketing thread) and avoid duplicate touches.
- HubSpot UX: if the signal history is hard to find, adoption drops. Ensure the Confluence doc includes a short “where to look” section with screenshots.

## Definition of done
- Confluence page published and linked in #general PSA.
- Stephanie (Sales), Markus (SE), Jasmine (CS), and David (Ops) sign off in comments.
- Training delivered and recorded; new-hire onboarding link added to Sales Enablement index.

title: Create regulated workloads landing page (ABM pilot)
preview:
Goal
Launch a customer-facing landing page tailored to security/compliance-driven buyers (finserv + healthcare/PHI-adjacent) as part of the 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot. Page should route to the correct next step (security review call / architecture consult) and link to Sales-ready security materials without making over-broad compliance claims.

Primary CTA
“Request a security + architecture review” (form) with secondary CTA: “See security FAQ” (public doc) and gated CTA: “Request evidence pack” (sales-assisted / NDA).

Scope / Requirements
1) Page structure (target: single page, lightweight, skimmable)
- Hero: regulated positioning (performance + compliance posture) with 1 sentence on deployment options.
- Sections:
  - “Deploy the way regulated teams need”: Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private (VPC/on-prem) with clear guidance on when to choose each.
  - “Security & compliance controls”: encryption, key management, audit logs, SSO/SAML, retention/residency controls (wording must be Security-approved).
  - “Operational reliability”: SLAs (where applicable), predictable throughput, observability, rollouts/fallback.
  - “How a 2–3 week pilot works”: benchmark + architecture validation + security review timeline.
  - “FAQ”: short, link out to full security FAQ.
- Footnotes/clarifiers:
  - No “HIPAA-certified/HIPAA-compliant” or “guarantees” language.
  - Clarify shared responsibility (customer config + Redwood controls).

2) Copy guidelines (must align with PMM + Security)
- Must use the approved phrasing from PMM/Security review thread (see: sources/gmail/soojin_lee/20250504-messaging-review-regulated-workloads.json).
- Avoid absolute statements unless backed by evidence pack artifacts.
- Keep claims to capabilities (e.g., “supports audit logging” vs “meets all audit requirements”).

3) Design + UX
- Reuse existing docs site layout patterns where possible.
- Include “Talk to Security” routing for regulated buyers (minimize dead-ends).
- Ensure accessibility (contrast, headings, keyboard nav). (Design ticket: DES-882)

4) Measurement / Ops
- Add UTMs and conversion tracking (form submission + calendar booking).
- Capture persona + deployment interest on form:
  - Persona: Security/GRC, Platform/ML Eng, Procurement/IT
  - Deployment interest: Dedicated, Private (VPC), On-prem, Not sure
  - Region/residency requirement (optional)
- Owner for attribution spec: Tyler Benson.

5) Review checklist (hard gate before publish)
Security (Dr. Aisha Rahman / Naomi Feldman)
- Approved language for encryption, key management, audit logging, retention/residency, incident response.
- Confirm links to Security FAQ + evidence pack index are correct and access-controlled.
- Confirm no new commitments on timelines/SLAs.

Legal (Michael Grant / Sofia Mendes)
- Check disclaimers/footnotes and CTA wording.
- Ensure evidence pack request flow implies NDA where required.

Docs (Elliot Price / Kira Thompson)
- Style, IA, link hygiene, and maintenance owner for future edits.

Deliverables
- Published landing page on docs/marketing site (final URL TBD; align with existing docs routing).
- Cross-links:
  - Security FAQ: sources/confluence/sales-enablement/security-faq/regulated-workloads-security-faq.json
  - Evidence pack index (internal + process): sources/confluence/security-and-compliance/compliance-evidence/abm-pilot-security-evidence-pack-index.json
- Brief internal enablement blurb in Slack (#sales, #marketing) with approved snippet + link.

Implementation notes / Links

text:
Goal
Launch a customer-facing landing page tailored to security/compliance-driven buyers (finserv + healthcare/PHI-adjacent) as part of the 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot. Scope / Requirements

  - “FAQ”: short, link out to full security FAQ.

  - Clarify shared responsibility (customer config + Redwood controls).

title: Pilot plan (NA): self-serve → sales handoff for PLG signals
preview:
## Context
We’re rolling out the first NA pilot of the PLG-to-sales signal routing pipeline + playbook. This ticket defines *who* gets included, *what guardrails* prevent spam/false positives, *how we measure impact*, and a *weekly review cadence* with Sales + Growth.

Pilot goals:
- Increase conversion from self-serve → sales-assisted evaluation (Dedicated/Private) without degrading customer trust.
- Validate signal thresholds + suppression rules (noise reduction) before expanding to other regions.
- Prove we can attribute outcomes back to signals (meeting booked, eval started, opp created).

Non-goals (pilot):
- Fully automated outbound sequences with no human review.
- Expanding beyond NA ownership/territories.

## Pilot scope (NA)
**Duration:** 3–4 weeks initial run, then decide expand/iterate.

**Sales coverage:** NA AEs + SE involvement on specific signals (see guardrails).

**Cohort:**
1) *Paid self-serve* accounts in NA (billing country/region NA; HubSpot region=NA)
2) *High-activation trials* (if we can reliably segment) where intent signals are strong (security/compliance docs) OR sustained usage is above cut line.

**Explicit exclusions:**
- Internal/test orgs and known partner/demo orgs (denylist from ENG-7801).
- Existing contracted Dedicated/Private customers (unless explicitly tagged as “expansion eligible” and owned by a CSM; see CS guardrails).
- Accounts currently in active sales cycle (HubSpot lifecycle stage: Opportunity/Negotiation) — route signal to timeline note only, do not create new tasks.

## Signal entry criteria (pilot cut lines)
We’ll start with conservative thresholds to minimize spam, then relax as we validate.

**A) Scale / spend velocity (threshold signals)**
- Sustained usage spike: >= 3 consecutive days above baseline (7d avg) by >= 2.0x AND absolute daily tokens exceed NA cut line (initial: 20M tokens/day; to be tuned based on pilot volume)
- Spend velocity: 7d spend growth >= 50% WoW AND 7d spend >= $1,500 (initial)
- Team expansion: >= 2 new workspaces in 14d OR >= 5 new org invites in 7d (initial)

**B) Reliability / capacity pain**
- Repeated 429s: >= 3 days in last 7 days with 429 rate > 1% OR >= 50 total 429 responses in 24h (initial)
- Latency/SLO regression: p95 latency exceeds profile target by >25% for 3 consecutive days (where available)

**C) Compliance / deployment intent**
- Security intent: >= 2 distinct security/compliance doc views within 7d (SOC2, audit logs, retention, SSO/SAML, KMS) *plus* either pricing page view or Dedicated/Private docs view
- Private/VPC intent: Private deployment docs view OR VPC keyword page view (or request form submit) within 14d

**Qualification score**
- Only route to task creation when score >= pilot threshold (initial: 70). Lower scores still append a timeline note (for visibility) but no task.

## Guardrails (anti-spam + customer trust)
**Routing suppression:**
- Per account suppression window for task creation: 14 days per signal family (Scale, Reliability, Compliance) — timeline notes OK.
- If marketing nurture is scheduled within next 48h for the same theme, defer AE task creation by 48h (avoid double-touch). (Coord with Camila/Tyler.)

**Ownership & dedupe:**
- Preserve existing HubSpot owner when present.
- If no owner:
  - Route to NA round-robin pool (Sales Ops config; Isabelle + David).
- If CSM exists and account is an existing paying customer with active support relationship:
  - Create a CSM notification task first (CS owns first touch unless explicitly marked “sales-led expansion”). (Per Jasmine’s guardrails.)

**SE involvement triggers (immediate):**
- Private/VPC intent
- Compliance intent with evidence requests (SOC2/audit logs/retention)
- Reliability pain where 429s are sustained and usage is above cut line

**Opt-out / complaint handling:**
- If customer requests no sales outreach, apply HubSpot “PLG Signal Outreach = Opted out” and suppress all future tasks; timeline notes only.
- If we create a false-positive outreach scenario, log as pilot incident and update thresholds/suppression rules. Reference SUP-3392 for comms template once available.

## Measurement plan (what we’ll track)
**Primary success metrics (weekly + cumulative):**
- Signal→task creation rate (by signal family)
- Contact rate (% tasks where AE logs “connected” or email reply)
- Meeting booked rate (within 14d of first task)
- Evaluation started rate (Dedicated/Private eval or benchmark run) within 30d
- Pipeline created ($) and stage progression (within 30d)

**Quality metrics / guardrail metrics:**
- False positive rate (AE marks “Not relevant” OR customer complaint OR disqualified reason = “no buying intent”) within 14d
- Duplicate touch rate (marketing + AE within 24h) and suppression effectiveness
- Volume per AE per week (target: <= 5 new signal tasks/week/AE during pilot)

**Attribution fields (HubSpot):**
- Add/confirm: “PLG Signal Type (latest)”, “PLG Signal Score (latest)”, “PLG Signal First Seen (date)”, “PLG Signal Last Routed (date)”, “PLG Signal Outcome”
- Outcome enums (minimum viable): Meeting booked | Eval started | Opp created | Disqualified | Suppressed (CS-owned) | False positive

**Baseline:**
- Compare against prior 4-week period for the same cohort (where possible) and/or holdout group (accounts that meet thresholds but are suppressed for pilot control).

## Pilot operating cadence
**Weekly 45-min review (NA):**
Attendees: Ben (owner), Stephanie (VP Sales NA), Avery (AE rep), Markus (SE lead), Tyler (Mktg Ops), David (HubSpot admin), Mei (analytics), Logan (telemetry) optional.

Agenda:
1) Volume & routing health: # signals, # tasks, rate limits/errors
2) AE feedback: top 5 high-quality + top 5 noisy signals
3) Threshold tuning proposals + go/no-go for changes
4) Outcomes: meetings/opps created; any churn risk or customer complaints
5) Action items + owners

**Change control:**
- Threshold/suppression changes only once per week after review (reduce thrash).
- Log any changes in this ticket + link to PR/HubSpot workflow update.

## Deliverables / acceptance criteria
- [ ] NA cohort definition documented (who’s in, who’s out) and communicated to Sales/Marketing/CS
- [ ] Guardrails implemented in HubSpot workflows and/or router suppression rules
- [ ] Measurement fields available in HubSpot + a simple dashboard (or report) for weekly review
- [ ] Weekly review meeting scheduled + agenda doc + action item log
- [ ] Pilot kickoff announcement in #sales + #marketing with expectations and feedback channel

## Links
- PRD: sources/confluence/product-docs/requirements/plg-to-sales-signal-routing-prd.json
- Schema ADR: sources/confluence/architecture-and-standards/decision-records/adr-usage-intent-signals-schema-v1.json
- HubSpot routing ADR: sources/confluence/architecture-and-standards/decision-records/adr-hubspot-routing-and-ownership-rules.json
- Working thresholds notes: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/signal-taxonomy-and-thresholds-working-notes.json
- Pilot cohort analysis: sources/google_drive/shared_drives/go-to-market/plg-signals-pilot-cohort-analysis.json

## Open questions
1) Trial inclusion: do we include trials at all in week 1, or start paid-only?
2) Holdout group: can we operationalize a control group without confusing AEs?
3) CS-first touch: confirm rules for paid self-serve customers with existing CSM ownership.
4) EMEA/EU edge cases: exclude anything with EU residency intent from NA pilot even if billing is US?

title: Regulated workloads landing page design (wireframes → hi-fi) for ABM pilot
preview:
Goal: Design a regulated workloads landing page to support the 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot (initially finserv + healthcare). The page must be credible for security/compliance-driven buyers, map clearly to Redwood Dedicated + Redwood Private, and convert to the right next step (security review call / architecture consult / pilot).

Primary audiences:
- Security / GRC (CISO, Security Engineering, Compliance): wants clear controls, auditability, and “no surprises” language.
- Platform / ML Engineering: wants deployment topology, performance, reliability, and integration details.
- Procurement / Vendor mgmt: wants packaging clarity and evidence request path.

Design requirements:
- Reuse Redwood marketing components where possible (hero, feature cards, accordion FAQ, CTA banner, two-column section layouts) to keep implementation fast and consistent.
- Support two adjacent narratives without building two separate pages: (1) Dedicated for predictable throughput + isolation, (2) Private (VPC/on‑prem) for regulated / restricted networking.
- Ensure compliance-safe language and visual hierarchy that makes “what we do / what we don’t claim” obvious.
- Accessibility: meet WCAG 2.1 AA for typography, contrast, focus states, and motion.

Success criteria:
- First scroll answers: “Can you deploy in my environment?”, “How do you handle auditability, encryption, retention?”, “What’s the next step?”
- Clear CTA and secondary CTAs (Evidence Pack request, Security FAQ, Pilot plan).
- Page structure is modular for future vertical variants (finserv / healthcare sections can be swapped or expanded without redesign).

title: Governance request orchestration for granular log redaction and export scenarios
preview: Create an orchestration and decision framework that intake, scores, and routes customer requests for log redaction, retention adjustments, PII handling, and audit exports. The goal is to reduce time-to-response for enterprise customers while ensuring every request follows the right legal/compliance path, required tech work, and SLOs. Deliverables: (1) triage scoring rubric and routing rules, (2) templated response flows for Support/CS/SEs, (3) engineering runbook stubs for implementation (API + console), and (4) intake automation design (Slack/Forms -> Linear -> gated escalations). Emphasis on minimizing manual review where safe (policy templates + automated redaction checks) while preserving legal hold and export fidelity when required.

title: Rollout plan + feature flag strategy for Console onboarding checklist (dogfood → cohorts → GA)
preview:
## Context
We are shipping a new in-Console onboarding checklist (“Guided onboarding + first production launch”) for Hosted API. This ticket defines:
- Rollout phases (internal dogfood → gradual cohort exposure → GA)
- Feature flag gating + cohort selection rules
- Kill switch criteria + operational monitoring
- Comms plan for Support/CS and internal stakeholders

Goal: de-risk launch and avoid creating new onboarding confusion or support burden while we validate step funnel instrumentation and checklist state correctness.

## Owners / stakeholders
- PM (Hosted API): Nikhil Sharma
- PM (Console): Marissa Cole
- Program sponsor: Alex Martinez
- Rollout + lifecycle messaging: Ben Carter (assignee)
- Console Eng: Dylan Brooks / Chloe Martin
- Telemetry: Logan Wright
- Support enablement: Dev Patel
- SRE review: Sean Gallagher

## What we are rolling out
### User-visible
- New “Get started” checklist module in Redwood Console
- Step details drawer + deep links to Console pages and docs
- Admin/support tools (override/reset) for troubleshooting (Support-only)

### Behind the scenes
- Event emission from gateway/control plane (server-side) to progress tracker
- Progress tracker API powering checklist state
- KPI dashboard + alerts (TTFS/TTFP + funnel)

## Feature flag strategy
### Flag names / surfaces
Primary exposure flag:
- `console.onboarding_checklist.enabled`

Supporting flags (if Eng prefers split for blast-radius control):
- `console.onboarding_checklist.ui_enabled` (pure UI)
- `onboarding_progress_service.enabled` (Console reads from service)
- `onboarding_events.emit_v1` (server-side event emission)
- `onboarding_admin_override.enabled` (Support-only)

### Default behavior
- Flag OFF by default across all tenants.
- When OFF:
  - Checklist module hidden (no empty state)
  - No user-facing changes in Console nav
  - Events MAY still emit (if we choose “dark launch” for telemetry) but must be safe + PII-free.

### Cohort selection rules (MVP)
Cohorting is done at the tenant/org level (not per-user) to avoid inconsistent experiences within an account.

Proposed targeting (ordered):
1) Allowlist (internal + design partners)
   - Redwood employees (dogfood tenants)
   - Explicit external tenants selected by PM/CS (design partners)
2) Gradual ramp for self-serve Hosted API tenants
   - Hash-based percentage rollout by `tenant_id` (e.g., 5% → 15% → 35% → 70% → 100%)
   - Only for “Hosted API / self-serve” plan to start
3) Expand to all new signups
   - Option A: show checklist for all new tenants created after a cutoff date (plus a percentage of existing)
   - Option B: continue hash-based ramp only (simpler)

Exclusions initially:
- Dedicated/Private tenants (until step content is validated and Console links are correct for those flows)
- Tenants in an active incident mitigation state (if we add a suppression toggle later)

### Kill switch / rollback
- Global kill switch via `console.onboarding_checklist.enabled = false`.
- Separate kill switch for event emission if needed (`onboarding_events.emit_v1 = false`) to avoid pipeline overload or data issues.
- Rollback should be possible without data migrations.

## Rollout phases
### Phase 0 — Internal readiness (pre-dogfood)
Exit criteria:
- KPI dashboard available and updating (freshness < 30 minutes)
- State monotonicity verified (no step regression from out-of-order events) — see ENG-5866
- Support playbook draft available (how to interpret checklist state)

### Phase 1 — Dogfood (Redwood employees)
Target:
- 2–3 internal tenants used by DevEx + Console teams
- Encourage running quickstart + streaming example in at least 2 SDKs (Python/TS recommended)

Duration:
- 3–5 business days (or until no P0/P1 bugs open)

Success criteria:
- No repeated step regressions for the same tenant
- Checklist UI stable (no broken deep links)
- At least 20 complete “first success” flows recorded end-to-end

### Phase 2 — External design partners (allowlist)
Target:
- 3–10 friendly customers (Support/CS coordinated)
- Include at least one account that previously hit 401/403 and one that hit 429 issues to validate mitigations

Guardrails:
- Support and PM notified before toggling on
- Dedicated Slack channel / shared thread for issues

Success criteria:
- Qualitative feedback that step order/copy is understandable
- No measurable increase in onboarding ticket rate from these accounts

### Phase 3 — Gradual cohort rollout (self-serve Hosted API)
Ramp proposal (adjustable based on incident risk):
- Day 1: 5%
- Day 3: 15%
- Day 5: 35%
- Day 7: 70%
- Day 10: 100%

Go/no-go at each ramp gate based on monitoring (below).

### Phase 4 — GA + comms
- Enable for 100% of eligible Hosted API tenants.
- Publish release notes + docs updates.
- Support macros updated and “what changed” internal blurb shared in #support and CS enablement channels.

### Phase 5 — Existing tenants backfill / experience parity (post-GA)
- Decide whether to show checklist to older tenants who already completed key steps.
- If we do: rely on backfill job (ENG-5844) + derived completion so checklist doesn’t look “stuck at 0%” for mature users.

## Monitoring + kill switch criteria
### Primary metrics (dashboard)
- TTFS (signup → first successful 2xx request) p50/p75
- TTFP proxy (first production traffic) as defined in PM-751
- Step funnel completion rates (view checklist → API key created → first 2xx → first streaming → retries/timeouts guidance acknowledged → usage dashboard visited → quotas/budgets configured → model pinned → fallback policy set)

### Data quality / pipeline health
- Event ingestion lag and drop rate
- % of tenants with checklist enabled but no events after 24h
- Cardinality checks (no high-card fields beyond schema constraints)

### Kill switch triggers (proposed)
Immediate kill switch (same day) if any of:
- Console error rate regression attributable to checklist rendering or progress API (e.g., +1% absolute 5xx on checklist endpoints)
- Progress service error rate > 1% for > 30 minutes in cohort
- Widespread step regressions (monotonicity violation) in > 2% of enabled tenants in a 24h window
- Support reports “checklist is blocking onboarding” (e.g., broken API key flow deep link) with multiple confirmations

Pause ramp (hold at current %) if any of:
- Onboarding-related support tickets per new tenant increase by > 20% vs baseline for 48h
- Funnel drop-off increases significantly at a new step (step completion -15% relative) and correlates with a UI/link issue
- Data freshness > 60 minutes for > 2 hours (dashboard unreliable)

## Support / CS comms plan
### Before Phase 2 (design partners)
- Share Support/CS briefing doc:
  - what the checklist is
  - what tenants will see
  - how to interpret partial completion
  - how to use admin override (once available)
- Add macros: 401/403 troubleshooting, streaming disconnects, 429/rate-limit handling, usage dashboard lag explanation

### During cohort rollout
- Weekly note (or per ramp gate) in #support + #customer-success:
  - current rollout %
  - known issues
  - links to runbook + “how to disable for a tenant” instructions

### GA communications
- Release notes: `sources/confluence/product-docs/release-notes/console-guided-onboarding-checklist-2025-04.json`
- Internal FAQ:
  - “Why does checklist say incomplete if we already use the API?”
  - “Why can’t I see it?” (cohorting)
  - “How do I reset it?” (Support only)

## Open questions
1) Do we want a “dismiss” option in MVP, or only auto-hide once complete? (Design leans auto-hide; Support prefers dismiss for mature users.)
2) Dark launch events for all tenants: are we comfortable with event volume prior to UI exposure? (Telemetry review needed.)
3) Cohorting policy for enterprises on Hosted API with procurement in-flight: should they be included or only allowlist? (CS input.)

## Decisions needed (for sign-off)
- Final ramp schedule (dates) and who can approve ramp gates (PM + SRE)
- Confirm kill switch thresholds + alert routing
- Confirm eligibility rules for “Hosted API self-serve” in flag targeting

- PRD: `sources/confluence/product-docs/requirements/guided-onboarding-first-production-launch-prd.json`
- UX spec: `sources/confluence/product-docs/design-specs/onboarding-checklist-console-ux-spec.json`

## Context
We are shipping a new in-Console onboarding checklist (“Guided onboarding + first production launch”) for Hosted API. This ticket defines:
- Rollout phases (internal dogfood → gradual cohort exposure → GA)
- Feature flag gating + cohort selection rules
- Kill switch criteria + operational monitoring
- Comms plan for Support/CS and internal stakeholders

Goal: de-risk launch and avoid creating new onboarding confusion or support burden while we validate step funnel instrumentation and checklist state correctness.

## Owners / stakeholders
- PM (Hosted API): Nikhil Sharma
- PM (Console): Marissa Cole
- Program sponsor: Alex Martinez
- Rollout + lifecycle messaging: Ben Carter (assignee)
- Console Eng: Dylan Brooks / Chloe Martin
- Telemetry: Logan Wright
- Support enablement: Dev Patel
- SRE review: Sean Gallagher

## What we are rolling out
### User-visible
- New “Get started” checklist module in Redwood Console
- Step details drawer + deep links to Console pages and docs
- Admin/support tools (override/reset) for troubleshooting (Support-only)

### Behind the scenes
- Event emission from gateway/control plane (server-side) to progress tracker
- Progress tracker API powering checklist state
- KPI dashboard + alerts (TTFS/TTFP + funnel)

## Feature flag strategy
### Flag names / surfaces
Primary exposure flag:
- `console.onboarding_checklist.enabled`

Supporting flags (if Eng prefers split for blast-radius control):
- `console.onboarding_checklist.ui_enabled` (pure UI)
- `onboarding_progress_service.enabled` (Console reads from service)
- `onboarding_events.emit_v1` (server-side event emission)
- `onboarding_admin_override.enabled` (Support-only)

### Default behavior
- Flag OFF by default across all tenants.
- When OFF:
  - Checklist module hidden (no empty state)
  - No user-facing changes in Console nav
  - Events MAY still emit (if we choose “dark launch” for telemetry) but must be safe + PII-free.

### Cohort selection rules (MVP)
Cohorting is done at the tenant/org level (not per-user) to avoid inconsistent experiences within an account.

Proposed targeting (ordered):
1) Allowlist (internal + design partners)
   - Redwood employees (dogfood tenants)
   - Explicit external tenants selected by PM/CS (design partners)
2) Gradual ramp for self-serve Hosted API tenants
   - Hash-based percentage rollout by `tenant_id` (e.g., 5% → 15% → 35% → 70% → 100%)
   - Only for “Hosted API / self-serve” plan to start
3) Expand to all new signups
   - Option A: show checklist for all new tenants created after a cutoff date (plus a percentage of existing)
   - Option B: continue hash-based ramp only (simpler)

Exclusions initially:
- Dedicated/Private tenants (until step content is validated and Console links are correct for those flows)
- Tenants in an active incident mitigation state (if we add a suppression toggle later)

### Kill switch / rollback
- Global kill switch via `console.onboarding_checklist.enabled = false`.
- Separate kill switch for event emission if needed (`onboarding_events.emit_v1 = false`) to avoid pipeline overload or data issues.
- Rollback should be possible without data migrations.

## Rollout phases
### Phase 0 — Internal readiness (pre-dogfood)
Exit criteria:
- KPI dashboard available and updating (freshness < 30 minutes)
- State monotonicity verified (no step regression from out-of-order events) — see ENG-5866
- Support playbook draft available (how to interpret checklist state)

### Phase 1 — Dogfood (Redwood employees)
Target:
- 2–3 internal tenants used by DevEx + Console teams
- Encourage running quickstart + streaming example in at least 2 SDKs (Python/TS recommended)

Duration:
- 3–5 business days (or until no P0/P1 bugs open)

Success criteria:
- No repeated step regressions for the same tenant
- Checklist UI stable (no broken deep links)
- At least 20 complete “first success” flows recorded end-to-end

### Phase 2 — External design partners (allowlist)
Target:
- 3–10 friendly customers (Support/CS coordinated)
- Include at least one account that previously hit 401/403 and one that hit 429 issues to validate mitigations

Guardrails:
- Support and PM notified before toggling on
- Dedicated Slack channel / shared thread for issues

Success criteria:
- Qualitative feedback that step order/copy is understandable
- No measurable increase in onboarding ticket rate from these accounts

### Phase 3 — Gradual cohort rollout (self-serve Hosted API)
Ramp proposal (adjustable based on incident risk):
- Day 1: 5%
- Day 3: 15%
- Day 5: 35%
- Day 7: 70%
- Day 10: 100%

Go/no-go at each ramp gate based on monitoring (below).

### Phase 4 — GA + comms
- Enable for 100% of eligible Hosted API tenants.
- Publish release notes + docs updates.
- Support macros updated and “what changed” internal blurb shared in #support and CS enablement channels.

### Phase 5 — Existing tenants backfill / experience parity (post-GA)
- Decide whether to show checklist to older tenants who already completed key steps.
- If we do: rely on backfill job (ENG-5844) + derived completion so checklist doesn’t look “stuck at 0%” for mature users.

title: Routing Governor: interactive Console sandbox for region/tier selection and fallback variants
preview: Build an interactive 'Routing Governor' sandbox in the Redwood Console that lets customers author, validate, and preview routing policies that express region selection, tier routing, and tiered fallback model variants. This feature surfaces validation hints, live cost/latency estimates, and a simulated traffic preview so product/platform teams can safely iterate on policies before applying them.

title: Per-tenant Commitment: Virtual Credit Buckets and Late Adjustment Billing PRD
preview: Implement a per-tenant "virtual credit" commitment mechanism to allow customers to pre-allocate committed compute/usage in flexible buckets that roll forward, support late adjustments, and produce clear invoice line items. This PRD defines product requirements, acceptance criteria, API contracts, billing reconciliation, migration paths for existing committed plans, and rollout controls.

title: Refactor sampling policy to bounded-emission + low-overhead tail detection
preview: Deliver a refactor of the tracing sampling layer to enforce a bounded span-emission budget per service while adding a low-cost tail-detection heuristic. Goal is to reduce CPU + network overhead from high-cardinality trace traffic without losing high-fidelity traces for tail latency and error analysis. This change should be safe for hosted and dedicated deployments, support config overrides per-customer, and include rollout/rollback tooling in Console.
